markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Inventory required Tiptap extensions and custom nodes for Handshake rich documents. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: TiptapExtensionInventory leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: TiptapExtensionInventory is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified feceb392: extension_inventory.test.ts 7/7 - typed Tiptap extension inventory. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: M1 mention/tag nodes uncreatable from the UI (commands inserted bare @/# text)
evidence.fix: mention.at / mention.tag commands take an arg-prompted value and insert REAL mention/tagMention nodes (id+label) + trailing space; empty values decline; reachable from palette and the new overflow menu (ED-LINK-002 creation path)
tests: editor_commands.test.ts: M1 mention/tag commands create REAL mention and tagMention nodes
RichTextEditor.test.tsx: overflow menu runs mention.at -> arg prompt -> debug nodeCounts.mention === 1

adversarial_review_v2.suites_observed: vitest 62 files/468 tests PASS (2 uncaught jsdom getClientRects errors from RichTextEditor.test.tsx command dispatch); playwright dependency lane 2/2 PASS, zero external requests

findings.title: Mention/tag nodes exist in schema but cannot be created from the editor UI
findings.evidence: extension_set.ts:127-146 configures Mention/tagMention suggestion with items only - no render UI, so typing @/# shows no popup and never inserts a node; editor_commands.ts:269-281 mention.at/mention.tag merely insertContent("@")/("#") literal text. ED-LINK-002 is marked BUILT in the capability inventory but the surface cannot mint a mention/tagMention node.
findings.fix: Configure suggestion.render (popup listbox over a real items source) or add direct insert commands with an arg prompt that insert {type:"mention"/"tagMention", attrs:{id,label}} nodes.

findings.title: Inventory advertises primitives with no instantiation-path test
findings.evidence: extension_inventory.ts declares every primitive incl. mention/tagMention; extension_inventory.test.ts asserts catalog shape only - no test builds the full editor and creates each primitive through a user-reachable path.
findings.fix: Add a per-primitive creation test over buildHandshakeEditorExtensions: for each inventory node, assert at least one command/input-rule/paste-rule can create it.

findings.title: StarterKit v3 marks (underline, strike) in schema but absent from command catalog/toolbar
findings.evidence: extension_set.ts uses StarterKit (v3 bundles Underline+Link); editor_commands.ts has no underline/strike/link-mark commands, so schema capabilities are unreachable.
findings.fix: Add format.underline/format.strike (and a plain-URL link.set/unset) commands to EDITOR_COMMANDS.

parity_gaps: ED-LINK-002 mentions/tags: downgrade BUILT -> PARTIAL (no creation UI)
Plain external URL hyperlink: schema mark present, no UI to set/unset/open (unowned)
missing_tests: creates a mention node through the @ suggestion popup (real editor)
creates a tagMention node through the # suggestion popup
every inventory primitive reachable via at least one command/rule

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-161 requires a concrete, restartable, machine-readable inventory of the Tiptap extensions, custom nodes/marks, typed wikilink kinds, and embedded Monaco surfaces the Handshake rich-document editor must ship, with each primitive mapped to its backend block kind and owning MT, rejecting sidecar-paper authority. The committed inventory (extension_inventory.ts) delivers exactly that as a typed product constant and cross-checks itself against the real MT-021 extension_set required-node contract so the two cannot drift; 7 tests pass.
findings: Commit feceb392 adds exactly two files: app/src/lib/editor/extension_inventory.ts (177 lines) and extension_inventory.test.ts (67 lines, 7 tests). Scope is clean: app/ only, no /.GOV/, no src/backend, no artifact dirs.

The inventory is a real typed constant, not a stub: WP009_EDITOR_PRIMITIVES (extension_inventory.ts:104-133) enumerates 22 concrete primitives (prose, table/task families, mention/tagMention, collaboration, hsLink typed wikilink, monacoCodeBlock, two monaco_surface entries), each with engine/kind/purpose/backendBlockKind/implementedBy. WP009_WIKILINK_KINDS (lines 80-92) enumerates the 11-prefix [[kind:value]] family with backend ref kinds. This satisfies acceptance_criteria 'names exact ... constraints, failure modes' (degradesGracefully flag, WP009_FATAL_PRIMITIVE_NAMES).

The anti-drift cross-check is non-vacuous: inventoryMissingRequiredNodes() (lines 168-171) filters the real WP009_REQUIRED_NODE_NAMES imported from ../tiptap/extension_set (the MT-021 file, confirmed present at app/src/lib/tiptap/extension_set.ts:76-85 listing table/tableRow/tableHeader/tableCell/taskList/taskItem/mention/tagMention) and the test (extension_inventory.test.ts:27-29) asserts it equals []. All 8 required nodes are present in the inventory, so the check truly couples the inventory to the implemented extension set rather than asserting against a self-authored constant.

Authority is correctly disclaimed: extension_inventory.ts:11-18 states durable authority is the versioned RichDocument JSON in PostgreSQL/EventLedger loaded/saved via /knowledge/documents, and this file is a typed product constant carrying NO sidecar research-paper authority. This satisfies the MT criterion 'rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof.' Because this MT claims no durable resource of its own, Spec-Realism sub_rule_2 (real managed-resource touch) is NA for an inventory MT.

Spec alignment confirmed against CURRENT bundle v02.194: 02-system-architecture.md:2694 (2.3.13.11 Project Knowledge Index and Rich Document Authority), :2707 (RichDocument = versioned ProseMirror/Tiptap JSON authority record), and :1583 (LM-LINK-002 inline @mentions as interactive links) match the inventory's node set, mention nodes, and Postgres/EventLedger authority note.

No deferred-live escape (sub_rule_1 PASS): grep/read of the impl and test shows no todo!/unimplemented!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-' on any reachable path; the file is pure data + pure functions, fully exercised by the unit tests.

The MT contract's adversarial_review_v2 MEDIUM finding (mention/tag nodes uncreatable from UI) targets editor_commands.ts / extension_set.ts (downstream MT-163 creation UI), not the MT-161 inventory deliverable, and is marked FIXED in commit 2949bc87. It does not impugn the inventory, whose job is to declare which primitives must ship; the inventory correctly lists mention and tagMention. Aside (non-blocking, expected per winding-down governance): the MT contract evidence references commit 2949bc87 and iteration-3 hardening for sibling MTs, but the MT-161 file itself was untouched after feceb392.
Aside (non-blocking): MT lifecycle.status is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and the validator_verdict is unset (no prior FAIL_V1 to re-verify), so this is a first-pass per-MT review.
evidence_checked: git show --stat feceb392 (wtc-kernel-009): exactly extension_inventory.ts + extension_inventory.test.ts

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
